behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions with descriptive error messages.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is parser-local validation/formatting logic, not module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is limited to invalid input parsing and error content ("rejects invalid expressions... with a descriptive error message").
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser function directly with invalid cron strings (month 13, day 32, malformed tokens) and assert structured error type/code plus message fragment per failure case.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard and cron edge semantics (last day of month, nearest weekday).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core risk is calendrical algorithm correctness inside next-time computation; can fail within one component. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess boundary required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration because behavior text targets calculator correctness itself, not parser/calculator wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-built schedule objects (or deterministic parsed fixtures), call next-run calculator from fixed start instants and assert exact next N timestamps for normal rules and edge rules (`L`, nearest weekday equivalent).

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules that fire within a configured minimum gap and reports conflict windows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure is at module boundary between schedule time generation and overlap-window detection (handoff of generated fire times and gap logic). Unit-only detector tests can miss representation/ordering mismatches. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess (so not System), no AI output dependency (not Agentic), no multi-agent chain (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser/next-run generator with overlap detector in-process: create two cron schedules whose occurrences are inside and outside a 5-minute gap, compute windows over a horizon, assert detected conflict intervals and non-conflicts.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when scheduler logic interacts with timezone/DST rules across modules (cron semantics + tz conversion). Unit-mocking timezone behavior can hide real DST boundary bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no subprocess/network/OS event dependency in behavior text (so not System), no AI dependency, no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because text specifies DST correctness, not real external infrastructure behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run engine components together with a real timezone rules library (e.g., `America/New_York`): assert spring-forward date resolves according to product rule (skip/shift) and fall-back date resolves ambiguity deterministically (first/second occurrence per spec).

behaviors.description: Engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill) when computed next run is in the past.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is policy decision at orchestration boundary (computed schedule state + job config + scheduler decision path). Testing a single helper risks missing handoff/state interpretation errors. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess, no AI dependence, no multi-agent sequence. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is local engine policy handling, not external infra.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test of scheduler service with deterministic clock and computed past-due next-run values: for each policy (`skip`, `backfill`), assert emitted decision/action set and updated next scheduled time/state.
